title: 10 possible side effects of Thuja occidentalis (arborvitae)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Thuja occidentalis causes allergic reactions when its essential oils come into contact with sensitive skin. This occurs because the plant contains terpenoids and phenolic compounds that can trigger an immune response in some individuals. These compounds stimulate the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to dilate and leads to symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling. As a result, people may experience adverse reactions when using thuja-based products or exposure to the plant itself.

Thuja occidentalis triggers skin irritation due to its terpenes and volatile oils, which can cause an allergic reaction or contact dermatitis. When used externally, these compounds can penetrate the skin and stimulate an immune response, leading to redness, itching, and inflammation.

Thuja occidentalis induces liver damage due to its ability to stimulate the liver and kidneys, causing increased levels of certain enzymes. This reaction can lead to inflammation and potentially scarring of liver tissue. Additionally, the oil's beta-thujone content may contribute to this toxic effect by disrupting normal liver function. As a result, some individuals may experience liver dysfunction or even severe liver damage when using Thuja occidentalis.

Thuja occidentalis increases bleeding risk due to its potential to interact with platelet aggregation and blood clotting factors. The essential oil of the plant has been shown to inhibit platelet activation and aggregation, which can lead to a decreased ability of blood to clot properly, thereby increasing the risk of bleeding. Additionally, Thuja occidentalis may also affect the body's natural anticoagulant mechanisms, further contributing to the increased risk of bleeding.

Thuja occidentalis worsens asthma symptoms due to its ability to cause bronchospasms and increase airway resistance. The terpenes present in the herb, such as alpha-thujone, can stimulate the respiratory system and trigger an asthmatic response in individuals with pre-existing conditions. This reaction may lead to wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, which can further exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Thuja occidentalis affects thyroid function due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and alter the body's hormone balance. The herb contains thujone, a compound that can mimic the effects of thyroid hormones in the body, leading to an overactive or underactive thyroid gland. This can result in symptoms such as changes in heart rate, weight gain or loss, fatigue, and mood swings.

Thuja occidentalis causes digestive issues in some individuals. This is due to the presence of thujone, a compound found in the essential oil of the plant, which can stimulate the nervous system and lead to an increase in stomach acid production. Additionally, Thuja's antimicrobial properties may interfere with gut bacteria, causing changes in bowel habits and discomfort. The result is often symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Thuja occidentalis triggers seizures due to its ability to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters. The essential oils present in Thuja can affect the levels of serotonin and dopamine, leading to an increased risk of seizure activity. Additionally, Thuja's effects on the nervous system may also contribute to this side effect, potentially causing changes in brain wave patterns and electrical activity that can lead to seizures.

Thuja occidentalis reduces blood pressure by inhibiting the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in regulating blood pressure. This mechanism can lead to a decrease in systolic and diastolic blood pressure levels. Additionally, Thuja's beta-blocker-like properties may also contribute to its ability to lower blood pressure by slowing down heart rate and reducing cardiac output. As a result, people using Thuja occidentalis may experience a reduction in their overall blood pressure.

Thuja occidentalis disrupts hormonal balance due to its ability to interact with estrogen receptors in the body. The essential oil extracted from the tree's needles contains a compound called thujone, which can mimic the effects of estrogen, leading to an imbalance in hormone levels. This may cause changes in menstrual cycles, hot flashes, and other symptoms related to hormonal fluctuations.

intro: Thuja occidentalis has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues like nausea and diarrhea, skin irritation, and allergic reactions. These adverse effects can occur due to the herb's toxic compounds, including thujone and alpha-thujone, which can cause phototoxicity and interact with other medications. For instance, if not properly used, Thuja occidentalis can worsen existing health conditions like liver or kidney disease, leading to severe complications that negatively impact daily life, such as decreased energy levels and increased susceptibility to infections.
intro_desc: Thuja occidentalis has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, skin irritation, and allergic reactions. These side effects are caused by overexposure to the herb's essential oils, particularly thujone. For instance, excessive consumption of Thuja tea can lead to stomach pain, nausea, and diarrhea, which can severely impair daily activities and overall well-being. In severe cases, skin contact with Thuja extracts can cause dermatitis, eczema, or even life-threatening anaphylaxis.
